sections.title: Fats
sections.narration: 29g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.
sections.subtitleText: 29g of fat. saturated fat is a major pressure point. for dairy, the fat side can either add richness or drag the whole thing down.

sections.title: Carbs
sections.narration: 4.1g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for dairy, the question is whether the payoff is worth the baggage.
sections.subtitleText: 4.1g of carbs. sugar, glycemic index, and starch are doing most of the work. for dairy, the question is whether the payoff is worth the baggage.

sections.title: Proteins
sections.narration: 38g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.
sections.subtitleText: 38g of protein. bioavailability and amino acid quality are both strong. useful protein can rescue a lot of weaker traits here.

sections.title: Vitamins
sections.narration: vitamin b12 at 44% daily value, vitamin a at 26% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.
sections.subtitleText: vitamin b12 at 44% daily value, vitamin a at 26% daily value stand out most. that is only mild support overall.

sections.title: Minerals
sections.narration: calcium at 92% daily value, zinc at 29% daily value, magnesium at 11% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.
sections.subtitleText: calcium at 92% daily value, zinc at 29% daily value, magnesium at 11% daily value stand out most. that is major mineral support.

sections.title: Pros
sections.narration: the upsides first: easy protein delivery for normal diets because dairy gets real credit for convenience. clear calcium identity because that is a core category strength. slots into meals very easily because practical versatility matters a lot for dairy.

sections.title: Cons
sections.narration: now the downsides: very dense and easy to overshoot because the nutrient density is real, but so is the concentration. fat quality varies wildly across dairy because some dairy is clean, some really is not. processing changes the category a lot because the label alone does not tell the whole story.

closing.summary: Parmesan does have some solid upside. but the tradeoffs keep it from climbing higher. best when the protein payoff or fermentation angle outweighs the fat and sodium tradeoffs.
